TextENGNew: The piece in which Rule Y — which has controlled this board from the shadows and never let itself be caught — is finally exposed in its entirety.
changeReason: Old translation calls it a 'game piece that uncovers' Rule Y; new translation correctly describes it as the piece 'in which Rule Y is finally exposed,' shifting the subject and meaning per the Japanese (ルールＹが全て暴き出されるカケラ).

TextENGNew: ...That impression is so powerful that it is easy to forget something equally important.
...We must not forget that Rule Z is utterly, completely broken within this piece.
changeReason: Old says the piece 'shows the path towards destroying Rule Z'; new says Rule Z 'is utterly, completely broken within this piece' — a significant difference between showing a path vs. being already broken (完膚なきまでに打ち破られた).

TextENGNew: Rule Z, which once made Rika Furude despair that it was the most hopeless of all fates, had ceased even to be an enemy for Keiichi Maebara and the others once they had learned everything.
They knew the right way to fight, and they fought within the rules of the game board.
And the victory earned through that — it is worth more than anything to us.
And then Keiichi Maebara shows me something.
changeReason: Old says Keiichi 'taught me a lesson' (past tense, pedagogical); new says he 'shows me something' (present tense, demonstrative) — different action and tense, matching the Japanese present-tense 示すのだ.

TextENGNew: There is no fate to which we must submit.
Only the power of belief can break through.
...The very fate that has crushed me again and again is itself built from Miyo Takano's power of belief.
...And so it stands to reason.
changeReason: Old says 'what he says is correct' (referring to a person being right); new says 'it stands to reason' (referring to logical consistency) — the Japanese だからそれは道理なのだ means 'therefore it stands to reason,' not that someone is correct.

TextENGNew: To overcome the power of belief, we need a power of belief that will not yield.
Strength of will can only be pierced by strength of will.
We overcame Rules X and Z and challenged the final Rule Y — but our pieces were not enough.
Each piece fought valiantly — but the enemy's pieces, led by Miyo Takano, were overwhelmingly superior.
She ground our pieces to dust so utterly that the effort itself was no consolation, and the game was reset to the very beginning...
And yet — we lost, but in our hearts we did not surrender.
Because at last, we had found the final enemy we must face, and we had come to understand how to fight.
changeReason: Old says 'found the true culprit, and also found a clue for how to fight'; new says 'found the final enemy we must face, and we had come to understand how to fight' — 'clue' vs 'understanding' is a meaningful difference in the degree of knowledge gained.

TextENGNew: With the pieces we have now, we cannot win.
Our power of belief is lacking. Our strength of will falls short.
But Hanyuu and I will begin again. As many times as it takes.
We will roll the dice again and again, searching for the best outcome, gathering pieces, and challenging anew.
...With a will strong enough to refuse defeat against Takano's.
Surely you, too, have come close to breaking more than once before reaching here...?
But you did not break — and here you are, having come this far alongside me.
changeReason: Old says 'you believed in me and followed me here' (attributing belief); new says 'you did not break — and here you are, having come this far alongside me' (attributing resilience) — different characterization of what the reader did, matching JPN 挫けず (not breaking).

TextENGNew: Just a little further... Let's keep going.
Let us create the final piece.
It will be a supremely happy, magnificent piece — one after which no further piece will be needed.
...The very last piece.
Now then — let us gather the pieces.
Let us gather miracles.
Your wishes, your feelings, your dreams — please, entrust them all to me...
